commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e85e78f473bc1ffe0e5005f17acfa40afe76b052)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds cron input recovery for concatenated local-llamacpp keys, adds normalization tests, and adds a CHANGELOG entry.

PR surface: Source +36, Tests +88, Docs +1. Total +125 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. as a source-level path: send the linked corrupted cron.add payload through the current cron.add handler and strict schema validation rejects the non-canonical keys. I did not run a live local-llamacpp model turn in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor and maintainer follow-up is needed because automation cannot supply the contributor's real local qwen/llama.cpp proof, and the remaining update-test/changelog cleanup should be reviewed on the PR branch.

target.title: fix(cron): recover from local-llamacpp parameter serialization bugs
target.body:
Local llamacpp models (e.g. qwen-27b) can serialize tool call arguments with concatenated JSON property names, causing cron add/update calls to fail with "unexpected property" errors.

Added `recoverConcatenatedKeys()` to `normalizeCronJobInput` that detects and splits these concatenated keys before the normal normalization pipeline runs.

## Corruption Patterns

| Intended | Corrupted | Pattern |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| `"name"` + `"payload"` (separate keys) | `"namePayload"` | Adjacent sibling keys concatenate |
| `"schedule"` → `{"kind": "every"}` | `"scheduleKind"` | Parent key merges with first nested child key |
| `"sessionTarget": "isolated"` | `"sessionTargetName": "evidence-test"` | Key merges with next sibling, value gets cross-wired |

## Fix

`recoverConcatenatedKeys()` handles three observed patterns:
1. `namePayload` → extracts `payload` object
2. `scheduleKind` → extracts `schedule` object (with nested kind intact)
3. `sessionTargetName` → extracts `name`, restores default `sessionTarget: "isolated"`

The recovery runs at the top of `normalizeCronJobInput`, before any field validation or coercion.

## Tests

Added 5 test cases covering:
- Individual pattern recovery (namePayload, scheduleKind, sessionTargetName)
- Full corruption scenario from issue #88439
- Normal (uncorrupted) passthrough verification

Fixes #88439
target.author: linux2010
